data.title: FedEx declares $1.45 dividend
data.description:
• FedEx (FDX) declares $1.45/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 1.57%

  • See FDX Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Medtronic gains FDA clearance for Stealth AXiS surgical system
data.description:
• The US FDA has granted clearance for Medtronic's (MDT) Stealth AXiS surgical system, which is used for spine procedures.
  • Medtronic noted that one of its key features is LiveAlign segmental tracking, an industry-first technology "that allows surgeons to visualize anatomic motion, surgical adjustments, and patient alignment in real time during spine surgery, without the need for repeated imaging. This capability helps reduce reliance on manual steps and workflow disruption, supporting more consistent execution of patient-specific surgical plans."
  • The company added that Stealth AXiS also streamlines workflows and allows for the exchange of information before, during, and after spine surgery.

data.title: Dividend Roundup: Chevron, Microsoft, Meta, Hyatt, and more
data.description:
This week's dividend activity included increased payouts from Phillips 66 (PSX) and Quest Diagnostics (DGX) as well as declarations from companies such as Meta (META) and Hyatt (H).

Additionally, looking towards next week, industry players such as Chevron (CVX) and Microsoft (MSFT) will see the ex-dividend dates for their upcoming dividend payments.

See a breakdown below of some of the highlights of this week, along with some upcoming activity on the dividend front.

Dividend Increases

Here are some companies that have announced a dividend increase in the past week:

T. Rowe Price (TROW)

  • Dividend declared: February 9
  • New dividend amount: $1.27
  • Previous amount: $1.30
  • Increase: 2.4%

Quest Diagnostics (DGX)

  • Dividend declared: February 10
  • New dividend amount: $0.86
  • Previous amount: $0.80
  • Increase: 7.5%

Phillips 66 (PSX)

  • Dividend declared: February 11
  • New dividend amount: $1.27

  • Increase: 5.8%

Dividend Declarations

Here are some companies that have announced a dividend in the past week:

Corning (GLW)

  • Dividend declared: February 11
  • Dividend amount: $0.28

Marriott (MAR)

  • Dividend declared: February 12
  • Dividend amount: $0.67

Meta (META)

  • Dividend declared: February 12
  • Dividend amount: $0.525

Hyatt (H)

  • Dividend declared: February 12
  • Dividend amount: $0.15

Upcoming Dividend Calendar

Here are some companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week:

Chevron (CVX)

  • Ex-dividend date: February 17
  • Payout date: March 10

ConocoPhillips (COP)

  • Ex-dividend date: February 18

Hasbro (HAS)

  • Ex-dividend date: February 18

Microsoft (MSFT)

Additional Dividend Information

For investors looking to gain further access to the dividend space, listed below are some popular dividend-focused ETFs that can be further analyzed:

Dividend ETFs: (VIG), (SCHD), (VYM), (DGRO), (SDY), (DVY), (IDV), (DGRW), (NOBL), (HDV), (RDVY), (VIGI), and (SDVY).

data.title: Las Vegas Sands names Patrick Dumont as its new CEO
data.description:
Las Vegas Sands (LVS) names Patrick Dumont as the company's chairman and chief executive officer. His appointment will be effective on March 1. Dumont will succeed Robert Goldstein, who becomes a senior advisor to the company through March 2028.

The move was not a surprise since the Las Vegas Sands' (LVS) board of directors had previously indicated Dumont would be the likely successor to Goldstein. Dumont was also appointed chairman of Sands China Ltd. (SCHYY) (SCHYF), the company's Hong Kong-listed subsidiary.

Dumont was appointed president and chief operating officer of Las Vegas Sands (LVS) in January 2021 and has been a member of the board of directors since 2017. He joined the company in 2010 and has focused on creating long-term growth for the business and its stakeholders in roles with responsibility for operations, properties, and capital allocation strategy. Dumont has more than 25 years of experience in management, development, operations, and corporate finance. Under his leadership, it was noted that the company has undertaken transformative investment programs in Macau and Singapore to elevate its product offerings, enhance customer experience, and increase long-term earnings potential.

Patrick Dumont is the son-in-law of Miriam Adelson, who is the top shareholder of Las Vegas Sands (LVS).

Shares of Las Vegas Sands (LVS) are down 12.7% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: Immunic rises after $400M private placement, leadership transition
data.description:
Immunic (IMUX) shares climbed ~26% in the premarket on Friday after the immunology drug developer announced a private placement to raise up to $400M and disclosed that its CEO, Daniel Vitt, has decided to step down.

As part of the private placement led by existing investor BVF Partners L.P., the New York-based biotech said it will issue roughly 229.1M pre-funded warrants, allowing investors to purchase shares of the company's common stock at $0.873 apiece, generating $200M upfront.

Immunic (IMUX) intends to raise up to $200M in additional gross proceeds through the issuance of warrants to buy approximately 229.1M shares at a strike price of $0.873 per share. The offering is expected to close on or about Feb. 17.

The company added that its existing cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities, along with proceeds from the offering, will help fund its operations into late 2027.

As for leadership changes, Immunic (IMUX) disclosed that its board of directors, along with CEO Vitt, will begin an executive search to find its next CEO who can steer the company “through its next stage of growth and into commercialization.”

Following the leadership shakeup, Vitt, a co-founder of Immunic (IMUX), will assume a new senior executive position while continuing to serve on the company’s board of directors.

Additionally, IMUX named Simona Skerjanec, a former Roche (RHHBY) executive, as the new interim chairperson of the board to replace Duane Nash, the former chairman.

The company also added Thor Nagel, Principal at BVF Partners, as a new member of the board ahead of a broader plan to replace as many as three directors in the lead-up to its 2027 annual meeting.

data.title: SA Sentiment: Which company will be the next to join the $1T club?
data.description:
With Walmart (WMT) becoming the first traditional retailer to achieve a $1T market capitalization, which company will be next?

Eli Lilly's (LLY) valuation hit $1T last November, making it the first pharma company to cross the threshold. Its market cap has since gone lower and is currently at $929.65B.

There are currently eight companies that have made it into the $1T market cap club, with several — like Nvidia (NVDA) and Apple (AAPL) — going on to hit more trillion-dollar milestones.

We asked Seeking Alpha readers in a recent Sentiment Survey which company will be the next to reach the $1T milestone.

Out of the nearly 900 responses, about 45% expect JPMorgan (JPM) to be next. JPM's market cap currently stands at $815.98B, so it still has some way to go. 

The next popular choice in the survey was Exxon Mobil (XOM), followed by Visa (V) and Johnson & Johnson (JNJ).

Here's a look at the market cap of the companies mentioned above over the past year:

data.title: Vale slips after posting widening quarterly loss, including $3.5B nickel assets impairment
data.description:
Vale (VALE) -1.9% pre-market Friday after reporting a Q4 net loss of $3.84B, compared to analyst expectations for a profit of $2.7B, widening its $694M net loss for the same period last year.

Vale (VALE) said it recorded a $3.5B impairment on Vale Base Metals' nickel assets in Canada, "triggered by a downward revision in long-term nickel price assumptions based on market estimates," and a writeoff of deferred tax assets from subsidiaries resulted in a $2.8B impact.

Q4 adjusted EBITDA climbed 21% Y/Y to $4.59B, matching expectations, which the company said was driven primarily by improved contributions from Vale Base Metals, and recurring free cash flow reached $1.7B, surging by $900M Y/Y, driven mainly by strong projected EBITDA and lower net financial expenses.

Q4 revenues rose 9% to $11.06B, surpassing the $10.86B analyst consensus estimate, as performance was solid across segments, as sales volumes for iron ore, copper, and nickel rose Y/Y by 5%, 8%, and 5%, respectively.

In the iron ore business, Vale (VALE) said it is advancing the commissioning of the Capanema and Vargem Grande 1 projects, with full production expected in this year's H1 and H2, respectively, and construction of the Serra Sul +20 project has reached 84% completion, with operations expected to begin in H2.

In base metals, Vale (VALE) said it has started construction of the Bacaba project to extend the operational life of the Sossego mining complex, with copper production estimated to average 50K tons/year over an eight-year mine life.

Last month, the company reported FY 2025 iron ore production had reached 336.1M metric tons, the highest annual level since 2018, including 90.4M tons in Q4, up 6% Y/Y.

For FY 2026, Vale (VALE) affirmed it expects to produce 335M-345M tons of iron ore.

data.title: Stock index futures little changed as inflation report comes in cooler
data.description:
Stock index futures were little changed on Friday as traders awaited key retail inflation data due out ahead of the opening bell.

Dow futures (INDU) were +0.1%, S&P 500 futures (SPX) and Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) were flat.

January Consumer Price Index: +0.2% M/M vs. +0.3% consensus and +0.3% prior, and +2.4% Y/Y vs. +2.5% consensus and +2.7% in December.

Core CPI (excludes food and energy): +0.3% M/M vs. +0.3% consensus and +0.2% prior, and +2.5% Y/Y vs. +2.5% consensus and +2.6% prior.

U.S. Treasury yields moved higher across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell to 4.08%, and the 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) was down 2 basis points to 4.71%. The shorter-end 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) fell 3 basis points to 3.42%.
